title: Coronations on the Dunes
title_candidates: Williams Lifts the Trophy, Mullin Slips on the Jacket
A Links Too Far for Meller
theme: On a wild, wind-blown links debut, Gregg Williams turns a five-point overnight lead into a twelve-point Trophy procession while David Mullin overhauls Jon Baker to claim the Green Jacket — all played out against Henry Meller's calamitous front nine and a Wooden Spoon that Stuart Neumann never threatens to relinquish.
opening_hook: Open on the early-hole carnage at Oitavos — Meller's front-six implosion (22 shots leaked, capped by a 10 at the 4th) setting the tone of a links that bit everyone, before zooming out to the two coronations it ultimately produced.
foreshadow: Mullin's bogey at the 2nd that quietly nudges him into the Green Jacket lead — a position he never relinquishes.
Meller's six-hole bloodbath to open the round, which all but seals the Spoon picture before the turn.
Williams's wobble at the 5th (a quad) and cold stretch 5-8 — a warning that ultimately proves a blip, not a collapse.

competition_state.name: Trophy
competition_state.note: WON by Williams. A five-point overnight lead over Jon Baker becomes a twelve-point margin over Alex Baker — Williams survives a quadruple bogey at the 5th and a cold 5-8 stretch, then rams home a 12-point purple patch from 12-15 (par, bogey, par, par) to put the title to bed. Jon Baker fades to third; Alex Baker climbs to runner-up despite a closing 9.

competition_state.name: Green Jacket
competition_state.note: WON by Mullin. Trailing Jon Baker by 2 at the start, Mullin nudged into the gross lead with a bogey at the 2nd and never gave it back — even a quadruple bogey at the 11th was absorbed, with birdies at the 13th and 16th stopping the rot. Final margin: six shots over Jon Baker.

competition_state.name: Wooden Spoon
competition_state.note: CLAIMED by Neumann. Started the day 22 points adrift on Stableford and, despite quads at the 5th and 17th, the gap to Meller closed to 11 — never close enough to matter. Spoon sealed.

players.arc: Wobbles through a quad at the 5th and a cold 5-8 stretch (11 shots leaked), then steadies and delivers a 12-point flourish through holes 12-15 to convert a five-point overnight lead into a twelve-point Trophy.

Edges into the Green Jacket lead with a bogey at the 2nd, weathers a quadruple bogey at the 11th, and uses birdies at the 13th and 16th to stop the bleeding — finishes six clear on gross for the Jacket.
Starts the day leading the Jacket by two and second in the Trophy by five; ends the round overhauled in both, slipping to third on Stableford and second on gross.
Climbs to Trophy runner-up despite a quadruple bogey at the 15th and a closing quintuple-bogey 9 at the 18th — propelled more by others' troubles than his own scoring.

Authors the round's signature catastrophe: 22 shots leaked over the opening six holes including a 10 at the 4th, a quintuple at the 5th and a quad at the 2nd, plus a closing 9-shot bleed from 16-18 — finishes fifth in the Trophy.
Quads at the 5th and 17th confirm the Wooden Spoon, though he does at least close the gap to Meller from 22 points to 11 over the day.

venue_notes: Oitavos Dunes — a debut for TEG, and the tour's second visit to the Lisbon Coast (2015). Arthur Hills's Atlantic links delivered exactly the sort of card-wrecking exposure its reputation promised: blowups across the field, scoring spread wide, and the early holes especially punishing.
